title: A 20-Year Chase: The Self-Taught Mechanic Behind a World-Leading Motorcycle
content:
For two decades, Zhang Xue grew from a teenage dropout into an aspiring entrepreneur who borrowed money just to stay afloat. A major turning point arrived when his company's Chinese-made motorcycle claimed victory at the Superbike World Championship. This historic win marked the first time a Chinese brand defeated dominant European and Japanese manufacturers in a top-tier competition. Having loved motorcycles since his youth, Zhang spent years as a stunt rider before pivoting to manufacturing. He firmly believed that building a championship-winning bike would be just as fulfilling as riding one.

Zhang gradually built his expertise by learning how to assemble components and managing the entire supply chain. Despite immense financial struggles, including borrowing from his landlord and friends to pay salaries, he founded his own brand. The winning motorcycle boasted significant advantages, such as a lightweight design and superior low-end torque. Furthermore, it cost only a fraction of the price of its European and Japanese competitors. Preorders immediately surged, breaking the long-standing perception that domestic brands were inferior.

Looking ahead, Zhang aims to erase any distinction in quality between imported and domestic motorbikes within a decade. He hopes his company will become an international top-tier brand recognized for its performance. Acknowledging that Chinese riders still lack a strong racing culture, he launched a support program with a massive prize pool. He also plans to establish a youth academy to train the next generation of competitive riders. Through his relentless dedication, Zhang is transforming both his destiny and China's motorcycle industry.
